$comment: C23 _BitInt(N) bit-precise integers (D-CSUBSET-BITINT), C1: N<=64 via a single native container + mod-2^N masking. Exercises + - * / % & | ^ << >> on _BitInt(4)/(6)/(17)/(40), int<->_BitInt conversions (CRIT-1: masked on conversion-to-_BitInt), sizeof/_Alignof _Static_asserts (size_t, const-folded — matching gcc-14/clang-19 x86-64 psABI), and the mod-2^N WRAP (CRIT-2: two 4-bit ops overflow 18->2). RED-ON-DISABLE: the exit 42 is load-bearing on the masking — a non-masking impl keeps the un-wrapped 18s and returns 74 (revert emitBitIntOp's bitIntMask call and this example returns 74, not 42). The _BitInt arithmetic never const-folds (const-eval is bit-precise-free until C4), so the debug arm runs every operator at runtime; the release arm folds them and must preserve the And-mask (still 42).
